C: This is the same entity structure used by players, objects, and items

C: Byte. Set to 0xFF for NO REF.

row_text: 0x07 | data_kind | Byte. Set to 0xFF for NO REF.

row_text: 0x1C | GXCallback Render Function | Always seems to be 0x80080E18(entity_t*, int code) for players and 0x8026EECC for items, called each frame
C: Always seems to be 0x80080E18(entity_t*, int code) for players and 0x8026EECC for items, called each frame

row_text: 0x20 | Render Priorities | 64-bit bitmask. Only valid for a CObj referencing GObj. Each bit determines which priority level is rendered by that camera.
C: 64-bit bitmask. Only valid for a CObj referencing GObj. Each bit determines which priority level is rendered by that camera.

C: Typically CObj, JObj, LObj, or Fog

row_text: 0x04 | Camera Type | Int. Index used by 803BCB18. 0 = Normal, 1 = Pause, 2 = One Player View (Off-Center), 3 = One Player View (Centered), 4 = Fixed, 5 = Crazy, 6 = Demo, 8 = Developer Locked
C: Int. Index used by 803BCB18. 0 = Normal, 1 = Pause, 2 = One Player View (Off-Center), 3 = One Player View (Centered), 4 = Fixed, 5 = Crazy, 6 = Demo, 8 = Developer Locked

row_text: 0x08 | Background Color | Byte. First 6 bits are hex color code
C: Byte. First 6 bits are hex color code

row_text: 0x1C | Tilt Angle 2? | Float. Only seems to tilt downward. Setting it causes the camera to jump to P1, if unpaused
C: Float. Only seems to tilt downward. Setting it causes the camera to jump to P1, if unpaused

row_text: 0x40 | Depth (Repeated) | Float. Upper Limit set by Stage Data.
C: Float. Upper Limit set by Stage Data.

row_text: 0x08 | ??? | 0x08 & 0x0C are either flags or garbage. Only set after something expires it seems.
C: 0x08 & 0x0C are either flags or garbage. Only set after something expires it seems.

row_text: 0x14 | Item Sub Type | 0x08 = Player Spawned? 0x4 = Game Spawned?
0x20 | ??? | Possibly flags. Seems to be set when an item has expired, like a laser.

row_text: 0x28 | State Index | 0 = On Ground, 2 = Held, 3 = Airborne (This index is used for 0xBC function pointers)

row_text: 0x34 | Projectile spin (amount per frame) | Float. 0.0 for Fox's Laser, 200.0 for Mario's Fireball and Link's bomb. Projectiles don't spin when set to 0. Setting 200 results in rotation of about 0.523599 radians (30 degrees) per frame. Didn't bother checking how this float is actually converted to radians. The check for this value occurs in zz_0274658
C: Float. 0.0 for Fox's Laser, 200.0 for Mario's Fireball and Link's bomb. Projectiles don't spin when set to 0. Setting 200 results in rotation of about 0.523599 radians (30 degrees) per frame.
